results.title: Night children
results.creator_credit: Bernard Devlin, Julian Biggs and National Film Board of Canada
results.brief_description: The story of a case worker with a Children’s Aid Society and the children she helps. Working round the clock, the Society receives appeals of every sort. The film shows how the Society follows through in the case of a little girl found wandering alone on downtown streets at night and in other cases of children abandoned, uncared for, the victims of their environment.

results.title: Nigeria: giant in Africa
results.creator_credit: Ronald Dick and National Film Board of Canada
results.brief_description: This vivid recounting of Nigeria’s history, from pre-European times to the mid-20th century, provides dramatic background to the political maturing of Africa’s most populous country. Of particular interest is the film’s treatment of ancient African cultures as revealed in the three regions that make up the Federation of Nigeria.

results.title: Musicanada
results.creator_credit: Malca Gillson, Tony Ianzelo, Tom Daly and National Film Board of Canada
results.brief_description: With no commentary other than the music and words of the performers themselves, this fast-moving film presents the grandest Canadian concert of them all. Here, the performers include both the great and the unknown from across the country, the musical styles span the centuries, and the artists are involved in all stages of musicianship: learning, teaching, conducting, recording, performing. Among the film’s many stars are Edith Butler, Beau Dommage, Maureen Forrester, Glenn Gould, Paul Horn, the Huggett Family, and Gilles Vigneault. (Award: San Francisco)

results.title: Cut your home heating costs
results.creator_credit: Barbara Anson, Dave Herman, Jeff Cox, John Hoskyns-Abrahall, Auska Videobooks and Rodale Press
results.brief_description: A two part program that looks at ways to cut your home heating costs. Pt 1. How to keep the heat in your house: Looks at where the most common energy leaks around the home are and how to halt heat loss with weather stripping, caulk, insulation, and window coverings. Pt 2. Wood heat: Examines how to start heating the home with wood. How to evaluate your home’s wood heating capacity, how to select the best stove, how to get more from your fireplace and how to maintain your stove safely are all examined. NB - forms for calculating energy use, mentioned in this video are not available in Australia.

results.title: Curly Top (Captioned)
results.creator_credit: Irving Cummings, Winfield Sheehan and Fox Film Corporation
results.brief_description: Since the death of their parents, Mary Blair and her little sister Elizabeth have been inmates of a private orphanage. Mary promised her dying mother that she and Elizabeth, nicknamed “Curly Top” would never be separated. With a natural talent for singing and dancing Curly Top is a mischievous yet appealing child. Elizabeth gets into trouble during a visit of the trustees, one of them, a playboy bachelor is taken with her and decides to adopt her. The bachelor soon discovers Mary is part of the ‘deal’ and this leads to some embarrassing and often funny predicaments. Stars Shirley Temple, John Boles, Rochelle Hudson and Jane Darwell.

results.title: Cultures of mothering
results.creator_credit: Robert de Young and Monash University
results.brief_description: Mothering is an issue that demands attention, but is often only brought in the public arena on the rather commercialised Mother’s Day. Mothering is a significant part of life that is not often considered as a separate issue for women. This program addresses mothering in Australian society and raises the level of debate about issues relating to mothering. Mothering is looked at over generations as well as its changing identity in contemporary society today. Mothering across cultures, the status of mothering, sexuality and mothering, as well as stereotypes are explored within this program, giving voice to a number of women who share their experiences of mothering. Produced by the Monash University Centre for Women’s Studies.

results.title: Culture Club: a kiss across the ocean
results.creator_credit: Keef, Hugh Symonds, Keefco Productions and Virgin Vision
results.brief_description: Recorded live at Hammersmith Odeon, December 1983. The charismatic Boy George and his band Culture Club perform some of their most loved songs in this very enjoyable and entertaining concert. The band, made up of Boy George, Jon Moss, Roy Hay, and Mikey Craig are supported by the fabulous Helen Terry as back-up vocal.

results.title: Cuckoo, Mr. Edgar!
results.creator_credit: Pierre M Trudeau, Therese Descary and National Film Board of Canada
results.brief_description: One mid-Spring evening at midnight, a cuckoo bird is struck by lightning and thrown from his perch to the ground. In the morning when the storm has cleared, he makes his way back up the tree to discover that his comfortable and tidy home has been filled with three unhatched eggs. Pretty soon the eggs have hatched and in their place emerge three young live chicks that become his surrogate. The life of the routinely organised bird is thrown into disarray as his affection for the three grows. A delightful animated tale for children about the pleasures of parenting. Voice provided by A. J. Henderson. Original music by Louis Gagne.
results.creator_credit_for_label: Pierre M Trudeau, Therese Descary and National Film Board of Canada

results.title: Cry sweet revenge = Uncle Tom's cabin
results.creator_credit: Géza von Radványi, Independent International Pictures and Kroger Babb
results.brief_description: Uncle Tom, Sambo, Simon Legree: their names are American bywords and all are characters in this acclaimed film version of Harriet Beecher Stowe’s immortal classic of American literature. This shocking indictment of slavery in the American South, is based on the book Abraham Lincoln credited with starting the Civil War. It is an unforgettable presentation of the peace loving Tom, forced to submit to a sadistic master, the evil Simon Legree. The helpless slaves are raped, tortured, humiliated and eventually driven to rebel against their oppressors. “Uncle Tom’s Cabin” accurately depicts the attitudes of white 19th Century Americans towards the institution of slavery and dramatizes, in heart-breaking detail, the tragic breakup of black Kentucky families sold “down the river.” It is a brutal, passionate indictment of American slavery and the explosive racial hatred that spawned it. Starring John Kitzmiller, Herbert Lom, Olivia Moorefield, Mary Ann Jensen and Prentiss Moulden.

results.title: Cry of the wild
results.creator_credit: Bill Mason, Bill Brind and National Film Board of Canada
results.brief_description: To hear Bill Mason tell about the making of this feature film (he is also the narrator) is to share with him his affection for his camera-quarry: the big northern timber wolves and pure-white Arctic wolves. What he brings to the screen is some unexcelled film footage and a great depth of lore about these wary, intelligent creatures of the Canadian wilds. Filming was done over three years in the Northwest Territories, British Columbia, the high Arctic, and near his Gatineau Hills (Quebec) home where he kept and observed three grown wolves and, eventually, the litter of cubs that provides some of the film’s most engaging sequences. Narration by Bill Mason.
